content: Just as the linguistic concept of 'reflexion' (reflection) is taught as a metacognitive skill for high school English learners to refine their writing by reviewing their own output against external communicative standards, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be re-designed to embed a classroom-style iterative reflexion routine, where the model acts as both the student producing an initial output and the teacher grading it against implicit rhetorical and accuracy benchmarks. This framework moves beyond existing single-perspective LLM reflection to replicate the human learning cycle of guided reflexive practice that language learners use.
